C[Drbobc: woopsa.. dont make moves at work!!!
C[Drbobc: bottom left group - too much! Well played!
thouis: Yeah, after G9 (move 106), Black has to choose between White getting a moyo in the upper left, or killing the bottom right.
thouis: Maybe not, it might just be dead after G9
C[Tom Newman: Have a good game.
Drbobc: Hi Tom.. have a good game...........
C[Drbobc: Hi Tom.. have a good game...........
C[Tom Newman: Thanks for the game. So far behind at this point I think I should resign. Sorry for dragging it out so far.
Drbobc: Hi.. have a good game...!
Drbobc: Hi.. have a good game...!
C[pathogenix: I'm at work, so I'll be sporadic :)
C[pathogenix: I didn't mean to play that :/ sorry
Drbobc: and you.. lomg time since we played!
Drbobc: and you.. lomg time since we played!
C[Ingrid: Yes, it's been ages!  Nice to play you again!
C[Drbobc: still playing go after all these years..
C[Ingrid: Yup!  Although I'm a bit rusty.  I had a baby and didn't play much at all for her first year.
Drbobc: baby = plenty of time at night when you're awake to play :)
Ingrid: Ha!  You said it.  I now have a tablet, so making moves in the middle of the night is all too easy...!
Drbobc: dont let her see it :)
Ingrid: Hee!  You sound as if you speak from experience...!  :)
C[Drbobc: I got two... they are fun at that age... when they hit teenage.... the game changes
C[Drbobc: but.. both my kids are great
Drbobc: its just when they were babies- they trained me not to sleep...........
Ingrid: Yes, we're still not sleeping.  It's slowly getting better, but I was totally unprepared for just how little sleep one gets in the early days...erm, months.
C[Drbobc: then your social life goes down the pan too
Drbobc: you can only have friends with babies.. no one else understands...
C[Ingrid: It's so true!  The babiless ones are disappearing!
C[Drbobc: and then of course there's guilt... shall I stay at home to care for them or send them to nursery and be  a """"good"" role model?
Drbobc: whichever decision you make is wrong! and when they hit teenage they will tell you :)
Drbobc: No one really knows guilt until they have children ~:)
C[Ingrid: Unnngh...can't we just skip the teenage years??!

C[Drbobc: ha...don't know.. still have them. The tough thing is that when they're younger it is hard to come to termms with thier dependancy.. when they hit teenage its hard to come to terms with thier growing lack of dependancy
Drbobc: The food thing is the kids changed me from being a research driven academic..into a human being :)

Ingrid: Hmm, yes, that certainly resonates.  We're still very much in parent shock mode.  I'm still staying at home with the little one and mostly loving it, but I also dearly miss using my brain...and sleeping...and having moments when I'm not 'on call'.  But maybe it all builds character...or humanity!  :)
C[Drbobc: there's no need to stop using your brain.. I found ""teletubbies"" to be  lot deeper than most people think.....
C[Ingrid: Ah...I've been missing a trick!
C[Drbobc: geeze.. younger son  (teenage) just been rejected by girl).. yet more parenting..
Ingrid: Oh, that sounds much more complicated than diaper changes...even the super messy ones that happen when you're away from home.
C[Drbobc: have you had the ""projectile vomit"" on the windsreen yet when your driving - a very alarming experience!
C[Ingrid: Eeek, no.  We've had projectile poo at the cinema, but I had a friend with me as backup.
C[Drbobc: ,ost have been some film.. was it in 3D?
C[Ingrid: Ha!  O, the horror...!  I've still not fully recovered...!
C[Drbobc: thought for the day...only 1500 nappies left
C[isaev: Sorry, it was a mistake. I was not going to take move back that way.
